name: adam-abundance
description: Switchboard: an orchestration + shared-memory (Postgres) console over the Valnet Auto portfolio (HotCars, CarBuzz, TopSpeed). FastAPI + server-rendered Jinja UI, Supabase-backed, Google-OAuth gated. Read-only by default with a human approval gate and a spend governor before any live action. Visible at /agents/<slug>/ once provisioned; workflow steps can call /api/data (JSON) or POST /run (text summary).

endpoints.description: The approval console UI (HTML). Visible at /agents/<slug>/ on shellagent.io. Login-gated (Google OAuth).
JSON aggregates of shared-memory + plan + spend state — for workflow consumption. No secrets, no raw editorial content.
Liveness probe. Public, no DB required.
Workflow step endpoint. Bearer-token auth (SWITCHBOARD_RUN_TOKEN). Body {"input": "..."} -> {"output": "..."}. Read-only status summary; never triggers actions or spend.

output_shape_from_api_data.rows: array — recent plans (id, brand, plan_date, status, items)
output_shape_from_api_data.summary: object — memory-by-type counts, active_flags, verified_facts, spend today

example_workflow: 2. Agent step: POST this agent's /run with {"input": "portfolio"}

runtime.note: Python app: run deploy/bootstrap.sh once (venv + pip install + alembic upgrade) before the first `pm2 start`. Do NOT run npm install / npm run build.

post_provision_notes: This is a Python/FastAPI app (not the Next.js scaffold it was created from). The deploy webhook should NOT run `npm install`/`npm run build`; instead run `deploy/bootstrap.sh` once, then `git pull && pm2 reload ecosystem.config.js --update-env` on each push.

Caddy strip_prefix routing: the app runs locally at / but Caddy serves it under /agents/<slug>/. Set APP_BASE_PATH=/agents/<slug> in .env so server-rendered links + redirects carry the prefix (handled centrally in app.py middleware — no per-template changes).
Config + secrets live in a repo-local .env (gitignored), loaded by ecosystem.config.js. Populate it from .env.prod.example. Never commit .env.
Database is Supabase Postgres via the pooler on port 5432; keep DB_STATEMENT_CACHE_SIZE=0 for asyncpg pooler compatibility.
Spend caps are OFF by default here (SPEND_CAPS_ENABLED=0) and Slack is intentionally NOT wired. Start with SWITCHBOARD_KILL_SWITCH=1 (observe/plan only) and flip live per-action when ready.
